1989	 * original target page was deleted.  We can be more strict, just not more
1990	 * lax.)
1991	 *
1992	 * Top level tree walk caller moves on to next page (makes it the new
1993	 * target) following recovery from this race.  (cf.  The rationale for
1994	 * child/downlink verification needing a ShareLock within
1995	 * bt_child_check(), where page deletion is also the main source of
1996	 * trouble.)
1997	 *
1998	 * Note that it doesn't matter if right sibling page here is actually a
1999	 * cousin page, because in order for the key space to be readjusted in a
2000	 * way that causes us issues in next level up (guiding problematic
2001	 * concurrent insertions to the cousin from the grandparent rather than to
2002	 * the sibling from the parent), there'd have to be page deletion of
2003	 * target's parent page (affecting target's parent's downlink in target's
2004	 * grandparent page).  Internal page deletion only occurs when there are
2005	 * no child pages (they were all fully deleted), and caller is checking
2006	 * that the target's parent has at least one non-deleted (so
2007	 * non-ignorable) child: the target page.  (Note that the first phase of
2008	 * deletion atomically marks the page to be deleted half-dead/ignorable at
2009	 * the same time downlink in its parent is removed, so caller will
2010	 * definitely not fail to detect that this happened.)
2011	 *
2012	 * This trick is inspired by the method backward scans use for dealing
2013	 * with concurrent page splits; concurrent page deletion is a problem that
2014	 * similarly receives special consideration sometimes (it's possible that
2015	 * the backwards scan will re-read its "original" block after failing to
2016	 * find a right-link to it, having already moved in the opposite direction
2017	 * (right/"forwards") a few times to try to locate one).  Just like us,
2018	 * that happens only to determine if there was a concurrent page deletion
2019	 * of a reference page, and just like us if there was a page deletion of
2020	 * that reference page it means we can move on from caring about the
2021	 * reference page.  See the nbtree README for a full description of how
2022	 * that works.
2023	 */ view japanese
2024	nline = PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(rightpage);
2025
2026	/*
2027	 * Get first data item, if any
2028	 */ view japanese
2029	if (P_ISLEAF(opaque) && nline >= P_FIRSTDATAKEY(opaque))
2030	{
2031		/* Return first data item (if any) */ view japanese
2032		rightitem = PageGetItemIdCareful(state, targetnext, rightpage,
2033										 P_FIRSTDATAKEY(opaque));
2034		*rightfirstoffset = P_FIRSTDATAKEY(opaque);
2035	}
2036	else if (!P_ISLEAF(opaque) &&
2037			 nline >= OffsetNumberNext(P_FIRSTDATAKEY(opaque)))
2038	{
2039		/*
2040		 * Return first item after the internal page's "negative infinity"
2041		 * item
2042		 */ view japanese
2043		rightitem = PageGetItemIdCareful(state, targetnext, rightpage,
2044										 OffsetNumberNext(P_FIRSTDATAKEY(opaque)));
2045	}
2046	else
2047	{
2048		/*
2049		 * No first item.  Page is probably empty leaf page, but it's also
2050		 * possible that it's an internal page with only a negative infinity
2051		 * item.
2052		 */ view japanese
2053		ereport(DEBUG2,
2054				(errcode(ERRCODE_NO_DATA),
2055				 errmsg_internal("%s block %u of index \"%s\" has no first data item",
2056								 P_ISLEAF(opaque) ? "leaf" : "internal", targetnext,
2057								 RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
2058		return NULL;
2059	}
2060
2061	/*
2062	 * Return first real item scankey.  Note that this relies on right page
2063	 * memory remaining allocated.
2064	 */ view japanese
2065	firstitup = (IndexTuple) PageGetItem(rightpage, rightitem);
2066	return bt_mkscankey_pivotsearch(state->rel, firstitup);
2067}
2068
2069/*
2070 * Check if two tuples are binary identical except the block number.  So,
2071 * this function is capable to compare pivot keys on different levels.
2072 */ view japanese
2073static bool
2074bt_pivot_tuple_identical(bool heapkeyspace, IndexTuple itup1, IndexTuple itup2)
2075{
2076	if (IndexTupleSize(itup1) != IndexTupleSize(itup2))
2077		return false;
2078
2079	if (heapkeyspace)
2080	{
2081		/*
2082		 * Offset number will contain important information in heapkeyspace
2083		 * indexes: the number of attributes left in the pivot tuple following
2084		 * suffix truncation.  Don't skip over it (compare it too).
2085		 */ view japanese
2086		if (memcmp(&itup1->t_tid.ip_posid, &itup2->t_tid.ip_posid,
2087				   IndexTupleSize(itup1) -
2088				   offsetof(ItemPointerData, ip_posid)) != 0)
2089			return false;
2090	}
2091	else
2092	{
2093		/*
2094		 * Cannot rely on offset number field having consistent value across
2095		 * levels on pg_upgrade'd !heapkeyspace indexes.  Compare contents of
2096		 * tuple starting from just after item pointer (i.e. after block
2097		 * number and offset number).
2098		 */ view japanese
2099		if (memcmp(&itup1->t_info, &itup2->t_info,
2100				   IndexTupleSize(itup1) -
2101				   offsetof(IndexTupleData, t_info)) != 0)
2102			return false;
2103	}
2104
2105	return true;
2106}
2107
2108/*---
2109 * Check high keys on the child level.  Traverse rightlinks from previous
2110 * downlink to the current one.  Check that there are no intermediate pages
2111 * with missing downlinks.
2112 *
2113 * If 'loaded_child' is given, it's assumed to be the page pointed to by the
2114 * downlink referenced by 'downlinkoffnum' of the target page.
2115 *
2116 * Basically this function is called for each target downlink and checks two
2117 * invariants:
2118 *
2119 * 1) You can reach the next child from previous one via rightlinks;
2120 * 2) Each child high key have matching pivot key on target level.
2121 *
2122 * Consider the sample tree picture.
2123 *
2124 *               1
2125 *           /       \
2126 *        2     <->     3
2127 *      /   \        /     \
2128 *    4  <>  5  <> 6 <> 7 <> 8
2129 *
2130 * This function will be called for blocks 4, 5, 6 and 8.  Consider what is
2131 * happening for each function call.
2132 *
2133 * - The function call for block 4 initializes data structure and matches high
2134 *   key of block 4 to downlink's pivot key of block 2.
2135 * - The high key of block 5 is matched to the high key of block 2.
2136 * - The block 6 has an incomplete split flag set, so its high key isn't
2137 *   matched to anything.
2138 * - The function call for block 8 checks that block 8 can be found while
2139 *   following rightlinks from block 6.  The high key of block 7 will be
2140 *   matched to downlink's pivot key in block 3.
2141 *
2142 * There is also final call of this function, which checks that there is no
2143 * missing downlinks for children to the right of the child referenced by
2144 * rightmost downlink in target level.
2145 */ view japanese
2146static void
2147bt_child_highkey_check(BtreeCheckState *state,
2148					   OffsetNumber target_downlinkoffnum,
2149					   Page loaded_child,
2150					   uint32 target_level)
2151{
2152	BlockNumber blkno = state->prevrightlink;
2153	Page		page;
2154	BTPageOpaque opaque;
2155	bool		rightsplit = state->previncompletesplit;
2156	bool		first = true;
2157	ItemId		itemid;
2158	IndexTuple	itup;
2159	BlockNumber downlink;
2160
2161	if (OffsetNumberIsValid(target_downlinkoffnum))
2162	{
2163		itemid = PageGetItemIdCareful(state, state->targetblock,
2164									  state->target, target_downlinkoffnum);
2165		itup = (IndexTuple) PageGetItem(state->target, itemid);
2166		downlink = BTreeTupleGetDownLink(itup);
2167	}
2168	else
2169	{
2170		downlink = P_NONE;
2171	}
2172
2173	/*
2174	 * If no previous rightlink is memorized for current level just below
2175	 * target page's level, we are about to start from the leftmost page. We
2176	 * can't follow rightlinks from previous page, because there is no
2177	 * previous page.  But we still can match high key.
2178	 *
2179	 * So we initialize variables for the loop above like there is previous
2180	 * page referencing current child.  Also we imply previous page to not
2181	 * have incomplete split flag, that would make us require downlink for
2182	 * current child.  That's correct, because leftmost page on the level
2183	 * should always have parent downlink.
2184	 */ view japanese
2185	if (!BlockNumberIsValid(blkno))
2186	{
2187		blkno = downlink;
2188		rightsplit = false;
2189	}
2190
2191	/* Move to the right on the child level */ view japanese
2192	while (true)
2193	{
2194		/*
2195		 * Did we traverse the whole tree level and this is check for pages to
2196		 * the right of rightmost downlink?
2197		 */ view japanese
2198		if (blkno == P_NONE && downlink == P_NONE)
2199		{
2200			state->prevrightlink = InvalidBlockNumber;
2201			state->previncompletesplit = false;
2202			return;
2203		}
2204
2205		/* Did we traverse the whole tree level and don't find next downlink? */ view japanese
2206		if (blkno == P_NONE)
2207			ereport(ERROR,
2208					(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2209					 errmsg("can't traverse from downlink %u to downlink %u of index \"%s\"",
2210							state->prevrightlink, downlink,
2211							RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
2212
2213		/* Load page contents */ view japanese
2214		if (blkno == downlink && loaded_child)
2215			page = loaded_child;
2216		else
2217			page = palloc_btree_page(state, blkno);
2218
2219		opaque = BTPageGetOpaque(page);
2220
2221		/* The first page we visit at the level should be leftmost */ view japanese
2222		if (first && !BlockNumberIsValid(state->prevrightlink) &&
2223			!bt_leftmost_ignoring_half_dead(state, blkno, opaque))
2224			ereport(ERROR,
2225					(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2226					 errmsg("the first child of leftmost target page is not leftmost of its level in index \"%s\"",
2227							RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2228					 errdetail_internal("Target block=%u child block=%u target page lsn=%X/%X.",
2229										state->targetblock, blkno,
2230										LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(state->targetlsn))));
2231
2232		/* Do level sanity check */ view japanese
2233		if ((!P_ISDELETED(opaque) || P_HAS_FULLXID(opaque)) &&
2234			opaque->btpo_level != target_level - 1)
2235			ereport(ERROR,
2236					(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2237					 errmsg("block found while following rightlinks from child of index \"%s\" has invalid level",
2238							RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2239					 errdetail_internal("Block pointed to=%u expected level=%u level in pointed to block=%u.",
2240										blkno, target_level - 1, opaque->btpo_level)));
2241
2242		/* Try to detect circular links */ view japanese
2243		if ((!first && blkno == state->prevrightlink) || blkno == opaque->btpo_prev)
2244			ereport(ERROR,
2245					(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2246					 errmsg("circular link chain found in block %u of index \"%s\"",
2247							blkno, RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
2248
2249		if (blkno != downlink && !P_IGNORE(opaque))
2250		{
2251			/* blkno probably has missing parent downlink */ view japanese
2252			bt_downlink_missing_check(state, rightsplit, blkno, page);
2253		}
2254
2255		rightsplit = P_INCOMPLETE_SPLIT(opaque);
2256
2257		/*
2258		 * If we visit page with high key, check that it is equal to the
2259		 * target key next to corresponding downlink.
2260		 */ view japanese
2261		if (!rightsplit && !P_RIGHTMOST(opaque) && !P_ISHALFDEAD(opaque))
2262		{
2263			BTPageOpaque topaque;
2264			IndexTuple	highkey;
2265			OffsetNumber pivotkey_offset;
2266
2267			/* Get high key */ view japanese
2268			itemid = PageGetItemIdCareful(state, blkno, page, P_HIKEY);
2269			highkey = (IndexTuple) PageGetItem(page, itemid);
2270
2271			/*
2272			 * There might be two situations when we examine high key.  If
2273			 * current child page is referenced by given target downlink, we
2274			 * should look to the next offset number for matching key from
2275			 * target page.
2276			 *
2277			 * Alternatively, we're following rightlinks somewhere in the
2278			 * middle between page referenced by previous target's downlink
2279			 * and the page referenced by current target's downlink.  If
2280			 * current child page hasn't incomplete split flag set, then its
2281			 * high key should match to the target's key of current offset
2282			 * number. This happens when a previous call here (to
2283			 * bt_child_highkey_check()) found an incomplete split, and we
2284			 * reach a right sibling page without a downlink -- the right
2285			 * sibling page's high key still needs to be matched to a
2286			 * separator key on the parent/target level.
2287			 *
2288			 * Don't apply OffsetNumberNext() to target_downlinkoffnum when we
2289			 * already had to step right on the child level. Our traversal of
2290			 * the child level must try to move in perfect lockstep behind (to
2291			 * the left of) the target/parent level traversal.
2292			 */ view japanese
2293			if (blkno == downlink)
2294				pivotkey_offset = OffsetNumberNext(target_downlinkoffnum);
2295			else
2296				pivotkey_offset = target_downlinkoffnum;
2297
2298			topaque = BTPageGetOpaque(state->target);
2299
2300			if (!offset_is_negative_infinity(topaque, pivotkey_offset))
2301			{
2302				/*
2303				 * If we're looking for the next pivot tuple in target page,
2304				 * but there is no more pivot tuples, then we should match to
2305				 * high key instead.
2306				 */ view japanese
2307				if (pivotkey_offset > PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(state->target))
2308				{
2309					if (P_RIGHTMOST(topaque))
2310						ereport(ERROR,
2311								(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2312								 errmsg("child high key is greater than rightmost pivot key on target level in index \"%s\"",
2313										RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2314								 errdetail_internal("Target block=%u child block=%u target page lsn=%X/%X.",
2315													state->targetblock, blkno,
2316													LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(state->targetlsn))));
2317					pivotkey_offset = P_HIKEY;
2318				}
2319				itemid = PageGetItemIdCareful(state, state->targetblock,
2320											  state->target, pivotkey_offset);
2321				itup = (IndexTuple) PageGetItem(state->target, itemid);
2322			}
2323			else
2324			{
2325				/*
2326				 * We cannot try to match child's high key to a negative
2327				 * infinity key in target, since there is nothing to compare.
2328				 * However, it's still possible to match child's high key
2329				 * outside of target page.  The reason why we're are is that
2330				 * bt_child_highkey_check() was previously called for the
2331				 * cousin page of 'loaded_child', which is incomplete split.
2332				 * So, now we traverse to the right of that cousin page and
2333				 * current child level page under consideration still belongs
2334				 * to the subtree of target's left sibling.  Thus, we need to
2335				 * match child's high key to its left uncle page high key.
2336				 * Thankfully we saved it, it's called a "low key" of target
2337				 * page.
2338				 */ view japanese
2339				if (!state->lowkey)
2340					ereport(ERROR,
2341							(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2342							 errmsg("can't find left sibling high key in index \"%s\"",
2343									RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2344							 errdetail_internal("Target block=%u child block=%u target page lsn=%X/%X.",
2345												state->targetblock, blkno,
2346												LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(state->targetlsn))));
2347				itup = state->lowkey;
2348			}
2349
2350			if (!bt_pivot_tuple_identical(state->heapkeyspace, highkey, itup))
2351			{
2352				ereport(ERROR,
2353						(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2354						 errmsg("mismatch between parent key and child high key in index \"%s\"",
2355								RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2356						 errdetail_internal("Target block=%u child block=%u target page lsn=%X/%X.",
2357											state->targetblock, blkno,
2358											LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(state->targetlsn))));
2359			}
2360		}
2361
2362		/* Exit if we already found next downlink */ view japanese
2363		if (blkno == downlink)
2364		{
2365			state->prevrightlink = opaque->btpo_next;
2366			state->previncompletesplit = rightsplit;
2367			return;
2368		}
2369
2370		/* Traverse to the next page using rightlink */ view japanese
2371		blkno = opaque->btpo_next;
2372
2373		/* Free page contents if it's allocated by us */ view japanese
2374		if (page != loaded_child)
2375			pfree(page);
2376		first = false;
2377	}
2378}
2379
2380/*
2381 * Checks one of target's downlink against its child page.
2382 *
2383 * Conceptually, the target page continues to be what is checked here.  The
2384 * target block is still blamed in the event of finding an invariant violation.
2385 * The downlink insertion into the target is probably where any problem raised
2386 * here arises, and there is no such thing as a parent link, so doing the
2387 * verification this way around is much more practical.
2388 *
2389 * This function visits child page and it's sequentially called for each
2390 * downlink of target page.  Assuming this we also check downlink connectivity
2391 * here in order to save child page visits.
2392 */ view japanese
2393static void
2394bt_child_check(BtreeCheckState *state, BTScanInsert targetkey,
2395			   OffsetNumber downlinkoffnum)
2396{
2397	ItemId		itemid;
2398	IndexTuple	itup;
2399	BlockNumber childblock;
2400	OffsetNumber offset;
2401	OffsetNumber maxoffset;
2402	Page		child;
2403	BTPageOpaque copaque;
2404	BTPageOpaque topaque;
2405
2406	itemid = PageGetItemIdCareful(state, state->targetblock,
2407								  state->target, downlinkoffnum);
2408	itup = (IndexTuple) PageGetItem(state->target, itemid);
2409	childblock = BTreeTupleGetDownLink(itup);
2410
2411	/*
2412	 * Caller must have ShareLock on target relation, because of
2413	 * considerations around page deletion by VACUUM.
2414	 *
2415	 * NB: In general, page deletion deletes the right sibling's downlink, not
2416	 * the downlink of the page being deleted; the deleted page's downlink is
2417	 * reused for its sibling.  The key space is thereby consolidated between
2418	 * the deleted page and its right sibling.  (We cannot delete a parent
2419	 * page's rightmost child unless it is the last child page, and we intend
2420	 * to also delete the parent itself.)
2421	 *
2422	 * If this verification happened without a ShareLock, the following race
2423	 * condition could cause false positives:
2424	 *
2425	 * In general, concurrent page deletion might occur, including deletion of
2426	 * the left sibling of the child page that is examined here.  If such a
2427	 * page deletion were to occur, closely followed by an insertion into the
2428	 * newly expanded key space of the child, a window for the false positive
2429	 * opens up: the stale parent/target downlink originally followed to get
2430	 * to the child legitimately ceases to be a lower bound on all items in
2431	 * the page, since the key space was concurrently expanded "left".
2432	 * (Insertion followed the "new" downlink for the child, not our now-stale
2433	 * downlink, which was concurrently physically removed in target/parent as
2434	 * part of deletion's first phase.)
2435	 *
2436	 * While we use various techniques elsewhere to perform cross-page
2437	 * verification for !readonly callers, a similar trick seems difficult
2438	 * here.  The tricks used by bt_recheck_sibling_links and by
2439	 * bt_right_page_check_scankey both involve verification of a same-level,
2440	 * cross-sibling invariant.  Cross-level invariants are far more squishy,
2441	 * though.  The nbtree REDO routines do not actually couple buffer locks
2442	 * across levels during page splits, so making any cross-level check work
2443	 * reliably in !readonly mode may be impossible.
2444	 */ view japanese
2445	Assert(state->readonly);
2446
2447	/*
2448	 * Verify child page has the downlink key from target page (its parent) as
2449	 * a lower bound; downlink must be strictly less than all keys on the
2450	 * page.
2451	 *
2452	 * Check all items, rather than checking just the first and trusting that
2453	 * the operator class obeys the transitive law.
2454	 */ view japanese
2455	topaque = BTPageGetOpaque(state->target);
2456	child = palloc_btree_page(state, childblock);
2457	copaque = BTPageGetOpaque(child);
2458	maxoffset = PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(child);
2459
2460	/*
2461	 * Since we've already loaded the child block, combine this check with
2462	 * check for downlink connectivity.
2463	 */ view japanese
2464	bt_child_highkey_check(state, downlinkoffnum,
2465						   child, topaque->btpo_level);
2466
2467	/*
2468	 * Since there cannot be a concurrent VACUUM operation in readonly mode,
2469	 * and since a page has no links within other pages (siblings and parent)
2470	 * once it is marked fully deleted, it should be impossible to land on a
2471	 * fully deleted page.
2472	 *
2473	 * It does not quite make sense to enforce that the page cannot even be
2474	 * half-dead, despite the fact the downlink is modified at the same stage
2475	 * that the child leaf page is marked half-dead.  That's incorrect because
2476	 * there may occasionally be multiple downlinks from a chain of pages
2477	 * undergoing deletion, where multiple successive calls are made to
2478	 * _bt_unlink_halfdead_page() by VACUUM before it can finally safely mark
2479	 * the leaf page as fully dead.  While _bt_mark_page_halfdead() usually
2480	 * removes the downlink to the leaf page that is marked half-dead, that's
2481	 * not guaranteed, so it's possible we'll land on a half-dead page with a
2482	 * downlink due to an interrupted multi-level page deletion.
2483	 *
2484	 * We go ahead with our checks if the child page is half-dead.  It's safe
2485	 * to do so because we do not test the child's high key, so it does not
2486	 * matter that the original high key will have been replaced by a dummy
2487	 * truncated high key within _bt_mark_page_halfdead().  All other page
2488	 * items are left intact on a half-dead page, so there is still something
2489	 * to test.
2490	 */ view japanese
2491	if (P_ISDELETED(copaque))
2492		ereport(ERROR,
2493				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2494				 errmsg("downlink to deleted page found in index \"%s\"",
2495						RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2496				 errdetail_internal("Parent block=%u child block=%u parent page lsn=%X/%X.",
2497									state->targetblock, childblock,
2498									LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(state->targetlsn))));
2499
2500	for (offset = P_FIRSTDATAKEY(copaque);
2501		 offset <= maxoffset;
2502		 offset = OffsetNumberNext(offset))
2503	{
2504		/*
2505		 * Skip comparison of target page key against "negative infinity"
2506		 * item, if any.  Checking it would indicate that it's not a strict
2507		 * lower bound, but that's only because of the hard-coding for
2508		 * negative infinity items within _bt_compare().
2509		 *
2510		 * If nbtree didn't truncate negative infinity tuples during internal
2511		 * page splits then we'd expect child's negative infinity key to be
2512		 * equal to the scankey/downlink from target/parent (it would be a
2513		 * "low key" in this hypothetical scenario, and so it would still need
2514		 * to be treated as a special case here).
2515		 *
2516		 * Negative infinity items can be thought of as a strict lower bound
2517		 * that works transitively, with the last non-negative-infinity pivot
2518		 * followed during a descent from the root as its "true" strict lower
2519		 * bound.  Only a small number of negative infinity items are truly
2520		 * negative infinity; those that are the first items of leftmost
2521		 * internal pages.  In more general terms, a negative infinity item is
2522		 * only negative infinity with respect to the subtree that the page is
2523		 * at the root of.
2524		 *
2525		 * See also: bt_rootdescend(), which can even detect transitive
2526		 * inconsistencies on cousin leaf pages.
2527		 */ view japanese
2528		if (offset_is_negative_infinity(copaque, offset))
2529			continue;
2530
2531		if (!invariant_l_nontarget_offset(state, targetkey, childblock, child,
2532										  offset))
2533			ereport(ERROR,
2534					(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2535					 errmsg("down-link lower bound invariant violated for index \"%s\"",
2536							RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2537					 errdetail_internal("Parent block=%u child index tid=(%u,%u) parent page lsn=%X/%X.",
2538										state->targetblock, childblock, offset,
2539										LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(state->targetlsn))));
2540	}
2541
2542	pfree(child);
2543}
2544
2545/*
2546 * Checks if page is missing a downlink that it should have.
2547 *
2548 * A page that lacks a downlink/parent may indicate corruption.  However, we
2549 * must account for the fact that a missing downlink can occasionally be
2550 * encountered in a non-corrupt index.  This can be due to an interrupted page
2551 * split, or an interrupted multi-level page deletion (i.e. there was a hard
2552 * crash or an error during a page split, or while VACUUM was deleting a
2553 * multi-level chain of pages).
2554 *
2555 * Note that this can only be called in readonly mode, so there is no need to
2556 * be concerned about concurrent page splits or page deletions.
2557 */ view japanese
2558static void
2559bt_downlink_missing_check(BtreeCheckState *state, bool rightsplit,
2560						  BlockNumber blkno, Page page)
2561{
2562	BTPageOpaque opaque = BTPageGetOpaque(page);
2563	ItemId		itemid;
2564	IndexTuple	itup;
2565	Page		child;
2566	BTPageOpaque copaque;
2567	uint32		level;
2568	BlockNumber childblk;
2569	XLogRecPtr	pagelsn;
2570
2571	Assert(state->readonly);
2572	Assert(!P_IGNORE(opaque));
2573
2574	/* No next level up with downlinks to fingerprint from the true root */ view japanese
2575	if (P_ISROOT(opaque))
2576		return;
2577
2578	pagelsn = PageGetLSN(page);
2579
2580	/*
2581	 * Incomplete (interrupted) page splits can account for the lack of a
2582	 * downlink.  Some inserting transaction should eventually complete the
2583	 * page split in passing, when it notices that the left sibling page is
2584	 * P_INCOMPLETE_SPLIT().
2585	 *
2586	 * In general, VACUUM is not prepared for there to be no downlink to a
2587	 * page that it deletes.  This is the main reason why the lack of a
2588	 * downlink can be reported as corruption here.  It's not obvious that an
2589	 * invalid missing downlink can result in wrong answers to queries,
2590	 * though, since index scans that land on the child may end up
2591	 * consistently moving right. The handling of concurrent page splits (and
2592	 * page deletions) within _bt_moveright() cannot distinguish
2593	 * inconsistencies that last for a moment from inconsistencies that are
2594	 * permanent and irrecoverable.
2595	 *
2596	 * VACUUM isn't even prepared to delete pages that have no downlink due to
2597	 * an incomplete page split, but it can detect and reason about that case
2598	 * by design, so it shouldn't be taken to indicate corruption.  See
2599	 * _bt_pagedel() for full details.
2600	 */ view japanese
2601	if (rightsplit)
2602	{
2603		ereport(DEBUG1,
2604				(errcode(ERRCODE_NO_DATA),
2605				 errmsg_internal("harmless interrupted page split detected in index \"%s\"",
2606								 RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2607				 errdetail_internal("Block=%u level=%u left sibling=%u page lsn=%X/%X.",
2608									blkno, opaque->btpo_level,
2609									opaque->btpo_prev,
2610									LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(pagelsn))));
2611		return;
2612	}
2613
2614	/*
2615	 * Page under check is probably the "top parent" of a multi-level page
2616	 * deletion.  We'll need to descend the subtree to make sure that
2617	 * descendant pages are consistent with that, though.
2618	 *
2619	 * If the page (which must be non-ignorable) is a leaf page, then clearly
2620	 * it can't be the top parent.  The lack of a downlink is probably a
2621	 * symptom of a broad problem that could just as easily cause
2622	 * inconsistencies anywhere else.
2623	 */ view japanese
2624	if (P_ISLEAF(opaque))
2625		ereport(ERROR,
2626				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2627				 errmsg("leaf index block lacks downlink in index \"%s\"",
2628						RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2629				 errdetail_internal("Block=%u page lsn=%X/%X.",
2630									blkno,
2631									LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(pagelsn))));
2632
2633	/* Descend from the given page, which is an internal page */ view japanese
2634	elog(DEBUG1, "checking for interrupted multi-level deletion due to missing downlink in index \"%s\"",
2635		 RelationGetRelationName(state->rel));
2636
2637	level = opaque->btpo_level;
2638	itemid = PageGetItemIdCareful(state, blkno, page, P_FIRSTDATAKEY(opaque));
2639	itup = (IndexTuple) PageGetItem(page, itemid);
2640	childblk = BTreeTupleGetDownLink(itup);
2641	for (;;)
2642	{
2643		CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
2644
2645		child = palloc_btree_page(state, childblk);
2646		copaque = BTPageGetOpaque(child);
2647
2648		if (P_ISLEAF(copaque))
2649			break;
2650
2651		/* Do an extra sanity check in passing on internal pages */ view japanese
2652		if (copaque->btpo_level != level - 1)
2653			ereport(ERROR,
2654					(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2655					 errmsg_internal("downlink points to block in index \"%s\" whose level is not one level down",
2656									 RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2657					 errdetail_internal("Top parent/under check block=%u block pointed to=%u expected level=%u level in pointed to block=%u.",
2658										blkno, childblk,
2659										level - 1, copaque->btpo_level)));
2660
2661		level = copaque->btpo_level;
2662		itemid = PageGetItemIdCareful(state, childblk, child,
2663									  P_FIRSTDATAKEY(copaque));
2664		itup = (IndexTuple) PageGetItem(child, itemid);
2665		childblk = BTreeTupleGetDownLink(itup);
2666		/* Be slightly more pro-active in freeing this memory, just in case */ view japanese
2667		pfree(child);
2668	}
2669
2670	/*
2671	 * Since there cannot be a concurrent VACUUM operation in readonly mode,
2672	 * and since a page has no links within other pages (siblings and parent)
2673	 * once it is marked fully deleted, it should be impossible to land on a
2674	 * fully deleted page.  See bt_child_check() for further details.
2675	 *
2676	 * The bt_child_check() P_ISDELETED() check is repeated here because
2677	 * bt_child_check() does not visit pages reachable through negative
2678	 * infinity items.  Besides, bt_child_check() is unwilling to descend
2679	 * multiple levels.  (The similar bt_child_check() P_ISDELETED() check
2680	 * within bt_check_level_from_leftmost() won't reach the page either,
2681	 * since the leaf's live siblings should have their sibling links updated
2682	 * to bypass the deletion target page when it is marked fully dead.)
2683	 *
2684	 * If this error is raised, it might be due to a previous multi-level page
2685	 * deletion that failed to realize that it wasn't yet safe to mark the
2686	 * leaf page as fully dead.  A "dangling downlink" will still remain when
2687	 * this happens.  The fact that the dangling downlink's page (the leaf's
2688	 * parent/ancestor page) lacked a downlink is incidental.
2689	 */ view japanese
2690	if (P_ISDELETED(copaque))
2691		ereport(ERROR,
2692				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2693				 errmsg_internal("downlink to deleted leaf page found in index \"%s\"",
2694								 RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2695				 errdetail_internal("Top parent/target block=%u leaf block=%u top parent/under check lsn=%X/%X.",
2696									blkno, childblk,
2697									LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(pagelsn))));
2698
2699	/*
2700	 * Iff leaf page is half-dead, its high key top parent link should point
2701	 * to what VACUUM considered to be the top parent page at the instant it
2702	 * was interrupted.  Provided the high key link actually points to the
2703	 * page under check, the missing downlink we detected is consistent with
2704	 * there having been an interrupted multi-level page deletion.  This means
2705	 * that the subtree with the page under check at its root (a page deletion
2706	 * chain) is in a consistent state, enabling VACUUM to resume deleting the
2707	 * entire chain the next time it encounters the half-dead leaf page.
2708	 */ view japanese
2709	if (P_ISHALFDEAD(copaque) && !P_RIGHTMOST(copaque))
2710	{
2711		itemid = PageGetItemIdCareful(state, childblk, child, P_HIKEY);
2712		itup = (IndexTuple) PageGetItem(child, itemid);
2713		if (BTreeTupleGetTopParent(itup) == blkno)
2714			return;
2715	}
2716
2717	ereport(ERROR,
2718			(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2719			 errmsg("internal index block lacks downlink in index \"%s\"",
2720					RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2721			 errdetail_internal("Block=%u level=%u page lsn=%X/%X.",
2722								blkno, opaque->btpo_level,
2723								LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(pagelsn))));
2724}
2725
2726/*
2727 * Per-tuple callback from table_index_build_scan, used to determine if index has
2728 * all the entries that definitely should have been observed in leaf pages of
2729 * the target index (that is, all IndexTuples that were fingerprinted by our
2730 * Bloom filter).  All heapallindexed checks occur here.
2731 *
2732 * The redundancy between an index and the table it indexes provides a good
2733 * opportunity to detect corruption, especially corruption within the table.
2734 * The high level principle behind the verification performed here is that any
2735 * IndexTuple that should be in an index following a fresh CREATE INDEX (based
2736 * on the same index definition) should also have been in the original,
2737 * existing index, which should have used exactly the same representation
2738 *
2739 * Since the overall structure of the index has already been verified, the most
2740 * likely explanation for error here is a corrupt heap page (could be logical
2741 * or physical corruption).  Index corruption may still be detected here,
2742 * though.  Only readonly callers will have verified that left links and right
2743 * links are in agreement, and so it's possible that a leaf page transposition
2744 * within index is actually the source of corruption detected here (for
2745 * !readonly callers).  The checks performed only for readonly callers might
2746 * more accurately frame the problem as a cross-page invariant issue (this
2747 * could even be due to recovery not replaying all WAL records).  The !readonly
2748 * ERROR message raised here includes a HINT about retrying with readonly
2749 * verification, just in case it's a cross-page invariant issue, though that
2750 * isn't particularly likely.
2751 *
2752 * table_index_build_scan() expects to be able to find the root tuple when a
2753 * heap-only tuple (the live tuple at the end of some HOT chain) needs to be
2754 * indexed, in order to replace the actual tuple's TID with the root tuple's
2755 * TID (which is what we're actually passed back here).  The index build heap
2756 * scan code will raise an error when a tuple that claims to be the root of the
2757 * heap-only tuple's HOT chain cannot be located.  This catches cases where the
2758 * original root item offset/root tuple for a HOT chain indicates (for whatever
2759 * reason) that the entire HOT chain is dead, despite the fact that the latest
2760 * heap-only tuple should be indexed.  When this happens, sequential scans may
2761 * always give correct answers, and all indexes may be considered structurally
2762 * consistent (i.e. the nbtree structural checks would not detect corruption).
2763 * It may be the case that only index scans give wrong answers, and yet heap or
2764 * SLRU corruption is the real culprit.  (While it's true that LP_DEAD bit
2765 * setting will probably also leave the index in a corrupt state before too
2766 * long, the problem is nonetheless that there is heap corruption.)
2767 *
2768 * Heap-only tuple handling within table_index_build_scan() works in a way that
2769 * helps us to detect index tuples that contain the wrong values (values that
2770 * don't match the latest tuple in the HOT chain).  This can happen when there
2771 * is no superseding index tuple due to a faulty assessment of HOT safety,
2772 * perhaps during the original CREATE INDEX.  Because the latest tuple's
2773 * contents are used with the root TID, an error will be raised when a tuple
2774 * with the same TID but non-matching attribute values is passed back to us.
2775 * Faulty assessment of HOT-safety was behind at least two distinct CREATE
2776 * INDEX CONCURRENTLY bugs that made it into stable releases, one of which was
2777 * undetected for many years.  In short, the same principle that allows a
2778 * REINDEX to repair corruption when there was an (undetected) broken HOT chain
2779 * also allows us to detect the corruption in many cases.
2780 */ view japanese
2781static void
2782bt_tuple_present_callback(Relation index, ItemPointer tid, Datum *values,
2783						  bool *isnull, bool tupleIsAlive, void *checkstate)
2784{
2785	BtreeCheckState *state = (BtreeCheckState *) checkstate;
2786	IndexTuple	itup,
2787				norm;
2788
2789	Assert(state->heapallindexed);
2790
2791	/* Generate a normalized index tuple for fingerprinting */ view japanese
2792	itup = index_form_tuple(RelationGetDescr(index), values, isnull);
2793	itup->t_tid = *tid;
2794	norm = bt_normalize_tuple(state, itup);
2795
2796	/* Probe Bloom filter -- tuple should be present */ view japanese
2797	if (bloom_lacks_element(state->filter, (unsigned char *) norm,
2798							IndexTupleSize(norm)))
2799		ereport(ERROR,
2800				(errcode(ERRCODE_DATA_CORRUPTED),
2801				 errmsg("heap tuple (%u,%u) from table \"%s\" lacks matching index tuple within index \"%s\"",
2802						ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(&(itup->t_tid)),
2803						ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&(itup->t_tid)),
2804						RelationGetRelationName(state->heaprel),
2805						RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
2806				 !state->readonly
2807				 ? errhint("Retrying verification using the function bt_index_parent_check() might provide a more specific error.")
2808				 : 0));
2809
2810	state->heaptuplespresent++;
2811	pfree(itup);
2812	/* Cannot leak memory here */
2813	if (norm != itup)
2814		pfree(norm);
2815}
2816
2817/*
2818 * Normalize an index tuple for fingerprinting.
2819 *
2820 * In general, index tuple formation is assumed to be deterministic by
2821 * heapallindexed verification, and IndexTuples are assumed immutable.  While
2822 * the LP_DEAD bit is mutable in leaf pages, that's ItemId metadata, which is
2823 * not fingerprinted.  Normalization is required to compensate for corner
2824 * cases where the determinism assumption doesn't quite work.
2825 *
2826 * There is currently one such case: index_form_tuple() does not try to hide
2827 * the source TOAST state of input datums.  The executor applies TOAST
2828 * compression for heap tuples based on different criteria to the compression
2829 * applied within btinsert()'s call to index_form_tuple(): it sometimes
2830 * compresses more aggressively, resulting in compressed heap tuple datums but
2831 * uncompressed corresponding index tuple datums.  A subsequent heapallindexed
2832 * verification will get a logically equivalent though bitwise unequal tuple
2833 * from index_form_tuple().  False positive heapallindexed corruption reports
2834 * could occur without normalizing away the inconsistency.
2835 *
2836 * Returned tuple is often caller's own original tuple.  Otherwise, it is a
2837 * new representation of caller's original index tuple, palloc()'d in caller's
2838 * memory context.
2839 *
2840 * Note: This routine is not concerned with distinctions about the
2841 * representation of tuples beyond those that might break heapallindexed
2842 * verification.  In particular, it won't try to normalize opclass-equal
2843 * datums with potentially distinct representations (e.g., btree/numeric_ops
2844 * index datums will not get their display scale normalized-away here).
2845 * Caller does normalization for non-pivot tuples that have a posting list,
2846 * since dummy CREATE INDEX callback code generates new tuples with the same
2847 * normalized representation.
2848 */ view japanese
2849static IndexTuple
2850bt_normalize_tuple(BtreeCheckState *state, IndexTuple itup)
2851{
2852	TupleDesc	tupleDescriptor = RelationGetDescr(state->rel);
2853	Datum		normalized[INDEX_MAX_KEYS];
2854	bool		isnull[INDEX_MAX_KEYS];
2855	bool		need_free[INDEX_MAX_KEYS];
2856	bool		formnewtup = false;
2857	IndexTuple	reformed;
2858	int			i;
2859
2860	/* Caller should only pass "logical" non-pivot tuples here */ view japanese
2861	Assert(!BTreeTupleIsPosting(itup) && !BTreeTupleIsPivot(itup));
2862
2863	/* Easy case: It's immediately clear that tuple has no varlena datums */ view japanese
2864	if (!IndexTupleHasVarwidths(itup))
2865		return itup;
2866
2867	for (i = 0; i < tupleDescriptor->natts; i++)
2868	{
2869		Form_pg_attribute att;
2870
2871		att = TupleDescAttr(tupleDescriptor, i);
2872
2873		/* Assume untoasted/already normalized datum initially */ view japanese
2874		need_free[i] = false;
2875		normalized[i] = index_getattr(itup, att->attnum,
2876									  tupleDescriptor,
2877									  &isnull[i]);
2878		if (att->attbyval || att->attlen != -1 || isnull[i])
2879			continue;
2880
2881		/*
2882		 * Callers always pass a tuple that could safely be inserted into the
2883		 * index without further processing, so an external varlena header
2884		 * should never be encountered here
2885		 */ view japanese
2886		if (VARATT_IS_EXTERNAL(DatumGetPointer(normalized[i])))
2887			ereport(ERROR,
2888					(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
2889					 errmsg("external varlena datum in tuple that references heap row (%u,%u) in index \"%s\"",
2890							ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(&(itup->t_tid)),
2891							ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&(itup->t_tid)),
2892							RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
2893		else if (!VARATT_IS_COMPRESSED(DatumGetPointer(normalized[i])) &&
2894				 VARSIZE(DatumGetPointer(normalized[i])) > TOAST_INDEX_TARGET &&
2895				 (att->attstorage == TYPSTORAGE_EXTENDED ||
2896				  att->attstorage == TYPSTORAGE_MAIN))
2897		{
2898			/*
2899			 * This value will be compressed by index_form_tuple() with the
2900			 * current storage settings.  We may be here because this tuple
2901			 * was formed with different storage settings.  So, force forming.
2902			 */ view japanese
2903			formnewtup = true;
2904		}
2905		else if (VARATT_IS_COMPRESSED(DatumGetPointer(normalized[i])))
2906		{
2907			formnewtup = true;
2908			normalized[i] = PointerGetDatum(PG_DETOAST_DATUM(normalized[i]));
2909			need_free[i] = true;
2910		}
2911
2912		/*
2913		 * Short tuples may have 1B or 4B header. Convert 4B header of short
2914		 * tuples to 1B
2915		 */ view japanese
2916		else if (VARATT_CAN_MAKE_SHORT(DatumGetPointer(normalized[i])))
2917		{
2918			/* convert to short varlena */ view japanese
2919			Size		len = VARATT_CONVERTED_SHORT_SIZE(DatumGetPointer(normalized[i]));
2920			char	   *data = palloc(len);
2921
2922			SET_VARSIZE_SHORT(data, len);
2923			memcpy(data + 1, VARDATA(DatumGetPointer(normalized[i])), len - 1);
2924
2925			formnewtup = true;
2926			normalized[i] = PointerGetDatum(data);
2927			need_free[i] = true;
2928		}
2929	}
2930
2931	/*
2932	 * Easier case: Tuple has varlena datums, none of which are compressed or
2933	 * short with 4B header
2934	 */ view japanese
2935	if (!formnewtup)
2936		return itup;
2937
2938	/*
2939	 * Hard case: Tuple had compressed varlena datums that necessitate
2940	 * creating normalized version of the tuple from uncompressed input datums
2941	 * (normalized input datums).  This is rather naive, but shouldn't be
2942	 * necessary too often.
2943	 *
2944	 * In the heap, tuples may contain short varlena datums with both 1B
2945	 * header and 4B headers.  But the corresponding index tuple should always
2946	 * have such varlena's with 1B headers.  So, if there is a short varlena
2947	 * with 4B header, we need to convert it for fingerprinting.
2948	 *
2949	 * Note that we rely on deterministic index_form_tuple() TOAST compression
2950	 * of normalized input.
2951	 */ view japanese
2952	reformed = index_form_tuple(tupleDescriptor, normalized, isnull);
2953	reformed->t_tid = itup->t_tid;
2954
2955	/* Cannot leak memory here */ view japanese
2956	for (i = 0; i < tupleDescriptor->natts; i++)
2957		if (need_free[i])
2958			pfree(DatumGetPointer(normalized[i]));
2959
2960	return reformed;
2961}
2962
2963/*
2964 * Produce palloc()'d "plain" tuple for nth posting list entry/TID.
2965 *
2966 * In general, deduplication is not supposed to change the logical contents of
2967 * an index.  Multiple index tuples are merged together into one equivalent
2968 * posting list index tuple when convenient.
2969 *
2970 * heapallindexed verification must normalize-away this variation in
2971 * representation by converting posting list tuples into two or more "plain"
2972 * tuples.  Each tuple must be fingerprinted separately -- there must be one
2973 * tuple for each corresponding Bloom filter probe during the heap scan.
2974 *
2975 * Note: Caller still needs to call bt_normalize_tuple() with returned tuple.
2976 */ view japanese
2977static inline IndexTuple
2978bt_posting_plain_tuple(IndexTuple itup, int n)
2979{
2980	Assert(BTreeTupleIsPosting(itup));
2981
2982	/* Returns non-posting-list tuple */ view japanese
2983	return _bt_form_posting(itup, BTreeTupleGetPostingN(itup, n), 1);
2984}
2985
2986/*
2987 * Search for itup in index, starting from fast root page.  itup must be a
2988 * non-pivot tuple.  This is only supported with heapkeyspace indexes, since
2989 * we rely on having fully unique keys to find a match with only a single
2990 * visit to a leaf page, barring an interrupted page split, where we may have
2991 * to move right.  (A concurrent page split is impossible because caller must
2992 * be readonly caller.)
2993 *
2994 * This routine can detect very subtle transitive consistency issues across
2995 * more than one level of the tree.  Leaf pages all have a high key (even the
2996 * rightmost page has a conceptual positive infinity high key), but not a low
2997 * key.  Their downlink in parent is a lower bound, which along with the high
2998 * key is almost enough to detect every possible inconsistency.  A downlink
2999 * separator key value won't always be available from parent, though, because
3000 * the first items of internal pages are negative infinity items, truncated
3001 * down to zero attributes during internal page splits.  While it's true that
3002 * bt_child_check() and the high key check can detect most imaginable key
3003 * space problems, there are remaining problems it won't detect with non-pivot
3004 * tuples in cousin leaf pages.  Starting a search from the root for every
3005 * existing leaf tuple detects small inconsistencies in upper levels of the
3006 * tree that cannot be detected any other way.  (Besides all this, this is
3007 * probably also useful as a direct test of the code used by index scans
3008 * themselves.)
3009 */ view japanese
3010static bool
3011bt_rootdescend(BtreeCheckState *state, IndexTuple itup)
3012{
3013	BTScanInsert key;
3014	BTStack		stack;
3015	Buffer		lbuf;
3016	bool		exists;
3017
3018	key = _bt_mkscankey(state->rel, itup);
3019	Assert(key->heapkeyspace && key->scantid != NULL);
3020
3021	/*
3022	 * Search from root.
3023	 *
3024	 * Ideally, we would arrange to only move right within _bt_search() when
3025	 * an interrupted page split is detected (i.e. when the incomplete split
3026	 * bit is found to be set), but for now we accept the possibility that
3027	 * that could conceal an inconsistency.
3028	 */ view japanese
3029	Assert(state->readonly && state->rootdescend);
3030	exists = false;
3031	stack = _bt_search(state->rel, NULL, key, &lbuf, BT_READ);
3032
3033	if (BufferIsValid(lbuf))
3034	{
3035		BTInsertStateData insertstate;
3036		OffsetNumber offnum;
3037		Page		page;
3038
3039		insertstate.itup = itup;
3040		insertstate.itemsz = MAXALIGN(IndexTupleSize(itup));
3041		insertstate.itup_key = key;
3042		insertstate.postingoff = 0;
3043		insertstate.bounds_valid = false;
3044		insertstate.buf = lbuf;
3045
3046		/* Get matching tuple on leaf page */ view japanese
3047		offnum = _bt_binsrch_insert(state->rel, &insertstate);
3048		/* Compare first >= matching item on leaf page, if any */ view japanese
3049		page = BufferGetPage(lbuf);
3050		/* Should match on first heap TID when tuple has a posting list */ view japanese
3051		if (offnum <= PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page) &&
3052			insertstate.postingoff <= 0 &&
3053			_bt_compare(state->rel, key, page, offnum) == 0)
3054			exists = true;
3055		_bt_relbuf(state->rel, lbuf);
3056	}
3057
3058	_bt_freestack(stack);
3059	pfree(key);
3060
3061	return exists;
3062}
3063
3064/*
3065 * Is particular offset within page (whose special state is passed by caller)
3066 * the page negative-infinity item?
3067 *
3068 * As noted in comments above _bt_compare(), there is special handling of the
3069 * first data item as a "negative infinity" item.  The hard-coding within
3070 * _bt_compare() makes comparing this item for the purposes of verification
3071 * pointless at best, since the IndexTuple only contains a valid TID (a
3072 * reference TID to child page).
3073 */ view japanese
3074static inline bool
3075offset_is_negative_infinity(BTPageOpaque opaque, OffsetNumber offset)
3076{
3077	/*
3078	 * For internal pages only, the first item after high key, if any, is
3079	 * negative infinity item.  Internal pages always have a negative infinity
3080	 * item, whereas leaf pages never have one.  This implies that negative
3081	 * infinity item is either first or second line item, or there is none
3082	 * within page.
3083	 *
3084	 * Negative infinity items are a special case among pivot tuples.  They
3085	 * always have zero attributes, while all other pivot tuples always have
3086	 * nkeyatts attributes.
3087	 *
3088	 * Right-most pages don't have a high key, but could be said to
3089	 * conceptually have a "positive infinity" high key.  Thus, there is a
3090	 * symmetry between down link items in parent pages, and high keys in
3091	 * children.  Together, they represent the part of the key space that
3092	 * belongs to each page in the index.  For example, all children of the
3093	 * root page will have negative infinity as a lower bound from root
3094	 * negative infinity downlink, and positive infinity as an upper bound
3095	 * (implicitly, from "imaginary" positive infinity high key in root).
3096	 */ view japanese
3097	return !P_ISLEAF(opaque) && offset == P_FIRSTDATAKEY(opaque);
3098}
3099
3100/*
3101 * Does the invariant hold that the key is strictly less than a given upper
3102 * bound offset item?
3103 *
3104 * Verifies line pointer on behalf of caller.
3105 *
3106 * If this function returns false, convention is that caller throws error due
3107 * to corruption.
3108 */ view japanese
3109static inline bool
3110invariant_l_offset(BtreeCheckState *state, BTScanInsert key,
3111				   OffsetNumber upperbound)
3112{
3113	ItemId		itemid;
3114	int32		cmp;
3115
3116	Assert(!key->nextkey && key->backward);
3117
3118	/* Verify line pointer before checking tuple */
3119	itemid = PageGetItemIdCareful(state, state->targetblock, state->target,
3120								  upperbound);
3121	/* pg_upgrade'd indexes may legally have equal sibling tuples */
3122	if (!key->heapkeyspace)
3123		return invariant_leq_offset(state, key, upperbound);
3124
3125	cmp = _bt_compare(state->rel, key, state->target, upperbound);
3126
3127	/*
3128	 * _bt_compare() is capable of determining that a scankey with a
3129	 * filled-out attribute is greater than pivot tuples where the comparison
3130	 * is resolved at a truncated attribute (value of attribute in pivot is
3131	 * minus infinity).  However, it is not capable of determining that a
3132	 * scankey is _less than_ a tuple on the basis of a comparison resolved at
3133	 * _scankey_ minus infinity attribute.  Complete an extra step to simulate
3134	 * having minus infinity values for omitted scankey attribute(s).
3135	 */ view japanese
3136	if (cmp == 0)
3137	{
3138		BTPageOpaque topaque;
3139		IndexTuple	ritup;
3140		int			uppnkeyatts;
3141		ItemPointer rheaptid;
3142		bool		nonpivot;
3143
3144		ritup = (IndexTuple) PageGetItem(state->target, itemid);
3145		topaque = BTPageGetOpaque(state->target);
3146		nonpivot = P_ISLEAF(topaque) && upperbound >= P_FIRSTDATAKEY(topaque);
3147
3148		/* Get number of keys + heap TID for item to the right */ view japanese
3149		uppnkeyatts = BTreeTupleGetNKeyAtts(ritup, state->rel);
3150		rheaptid = BTreeTupleGetHeapTIDCareful(state, ritup, nonpivot);
3151
3152		/* Heap TID is tiebreaker key attribute */
3153		if (key->keysz == uppnkeyatts)
3154			return key->scantid == NULL && rheaptid != NULL;
3155
3156		return key->keysz < uppnkeyatts;
3157	}
3158
3159	return cmp < 0;
3160}
3161
3162/*
3163 * Does the invariant hold that the key is less than or equal to a given upper
3164 * bound offset item?
3165 *
3166 * Caller should have verified that upperbound's line pointer is consistent
3167 * using PageGetItemIdCareful() call.
3168 *
3169 * If this function returns false, convention is that caller throws error due
3170 * to corruption.
3171 */ view japanese
3172static inline bool
3173invariant_leq_offset(BtreeCheckState *state, BTScanInsert key,
3174					 OffsetNumber upperbound)
3175{
3176	int32		cmp;
3177
3178	Assert(!key->nextkey && key->backward);
3179
3180	cmp = _bt_compare(state->rel, key, state->target, upperbound);
3181
3182	return cmp <= 0;
3183}
3184
3185/*
3186 * Does the invariant hold that the key is strictly greater than a given lower
3187 * bound offset item?
3188 *
3189 * Caller should have verified that lowerbound's line pointer is consistent
3190 * using PageGetItemIdCareful() call.
3191 *
3192 * If this function returns false, convention is that caller throws error due
3193 * to corruption.
3194 */ view japanese
3195static inline bool
3196invariant_g_offset(BtreeCheckState *state, BTScanInsert key,
3197				   OffsetNumber lowerbound)
3198{
3199	int32		cmp;
3200
3201	Assert(!key->nextkey && key->backward);
3202
3203	cmp = _bt_compare(state->rel, key, state->target, lowerbound);
3204
3205	/* pg_upgrade'd indexes may legally have equal sibling tuples */
3206	if (!key->heapkeyspace)
3207		return cmp >= 0;
3208
3209	/*
3210	 * No need to consider the possibility that scankey has attributes that we
3211	 * need to force to be interpreted as negative infinity.  _bt_compare() is
3212	 * able to determine that scankey is greater than negative infinity.  The
3213	 * distinction between "==" and "<" isn't interesting here, since
3214	 * corruption is indicated either way.
3215	 */ view japanese
3216	return cmp > 0;
3217}
3218
3219/*
3220 * Does the invariant hold that the key is strictly less than a given upper
3221 * bound offset item, with the offset relating to a caller-supplied page that
3222 * is not the current target page?
3223 *
3224 * Caller's non-target page is a child page of the target, checked as part of
3225 * checking a property of the target page (i.e. the key comes from the
3226 * target).  Verifies line pointer on behalf of caller.
3227 *
3228 * If this function returns false, convention is that caller throws error due
3229 * to corruption.
3230 */ view japanese
3231static inline bool
3232invariant_l_nontarget_offset(BtreeCheckState *state, BTScanInsert key,
3233							 BlockNumber nontargetblock, Page nontarget,
3234							 OffsetNumber upperbound)
3235{
3236	ItemId		itemid;
3237	int32		cmp;
3238
3239	Assert(!key->nextkey && key->backward);
3240
3241	/* Verify line pointer before checking tuple */ view japanese
3242	itemid = PageGetItemIdCareful(state, nontargetblock, nontarget,
3243								  upperbound);
3244	cmp = _bt_compare(state->rel, key, nontarget, upperbound);
3245
3246	/* pg_upgrade'd indexes may legally have equal sibling tuples */ view japanese
3247	if (!key->heapkeyspace)
3248		return cmp <= 0;
3249
3250	/* See invariant_l_offset() for an explanation of this extra step */ view japanese
3251	if (cmp == 0)
3252	{
3253		IndexTuple	child;
3254		int			uppnkeyatts;
3255		ItemPointer childheaptid;
3256		BTPageOpaque copaque;
3257		bool		nonpivot;
3258
3259		child = (IndexTuple) PageGetItem(nontarget, itemid);
3260		copaque = BTPageGetOpaque(nontarget);
3261		nonpivot = P_ISLEAF(copaque) && upperbound >= P_FIRSTDATAKEY(copaque);
3262
3263		/* Get number of keys + heap TID for child/non-target item */ view japanese
3264		uppnkeyatts = BTreeTupleGetNKeyAtts(child, state->rel);
3265		childheaptid = BTreeTupleGetHeapTIDCareful(state, child, nonpivot);
3266
3267		/* Heap TID is tiebreaker key attribute */ view japanese
3268		if (key->keysz == uppnkeyatts)
3269			return key->scantid == NULL && childheaptid != NULL;
3270
3271		return key->keysz < uppnkeyatts;
3272	}
3273
3274	return cmp < 0;
3275}
3276
3277/*
3278 * Given a block number of a B-Tree page, return page in palloc()'d memory.
3279 * While at it, perform some basic checks of the page.
3280 *
3281 * There is never an attempt to get a consistent view of multiple pages using
3282 * multiple concurrent buffer locks; in general, we only acquire a single pin
3283 * and buffer lock at a time, which is often all that the nbtree code requires.
3284 * (Actually, bt_recheck_sibling_links couples buffer locks, which is the only
3285 * exception to this general rule.)
3286 *
3287 * Operating on a copy of the page is useful because it prevents control
3288 * getting stuck in an uninterruptible state when an underlying operator class
3289 * misbehaves.
3290 */ view japanese
3291static Page
3292palloc_btree_page(BtreeCheckState *state, BlockNumber blocknum)
3293{
3294	Buffer		buffer;
3295	Page		page;
3296	BTPageOpaque opaque;
3297	OffsetNumber maxoffset;
3298
3299	page = palloc(BLCKSZ);
3300
3301	/*
3302	 * We copy the page into local storage to avoid holding pin on the buffer
3303	 * longer than we must.
3304	 */ view japanese
3305	buffer = ReadBufferExtended(state->rel, MAIN_FORKNUM, blocknum, RBM_NORMAL,
3306								state->checkstrategy);
3307	LockBuffer(buffer, BT_READ);
3308
3309	/*
3310	 * Perform the same basic sanity checking that nbtree itself performs for
3311	 * every page:
3312	 */ view japanese
3313	_bt_checkpage(state->rel, buffer);
3314
3315	/* Only use copy of page in palloc()'d memory */ view japanese
3316	memcpy(page, BufferGetPage(buffer), BLCKSZ);
3317	UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
3318
3319	opaque = BTPageGetOpaque(page);
3320
3321	if (P_ISMETA(opaque) && blocknum != BTREE_METAPAGE)
3322		ereport(ERROR,
3323				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3324				 errmsg("invalid meta page found at block %u in index \"%s\"",
3325						blocknum, RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
3326
3327	/* Check page from block that ought to be meta page */ view japanese
3328	if (blocknum == BTREE_METAPAGE)
3329	{
3330		BTMetaPageData *metad = BTPageGetMeta(page);
3331
3332		if (!P_ISMETA(opaque) ||
3333			metad->btm_magic != BTREE_MAGIC)
3334			ereport(ERROR,
3335					(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3336					 errmsg("index \"%s\" meta page is corrupt",
3337							RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
3338
3339		if (metad->btm_version < BTREE_MIN_VERSION ||
3340			metad->btm_version > BTREE_VERSION)
3341			ereport(ERROR,
3342					(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3343					 errmsg("version mismatch in index \"%s\": file version %d, "
3344							"current version %d, minimum supported version %d",
3345							RelationGetRelationName(state->rel),
3346							metad->btm_version, BTREE_VERSION,
3347							BTREE_MIN_VERSION)));
3348
3349		/* Finished with metapage checks */ view japanese
3350		return page;
3351	}
3352
3353	/*
3354	 * Deleted pages that still use the old 32-bit XID representation have no
3355	 * sane "level" field because they type pun the field, but all other pages
3356	 * (including pages deleted on Postgres 14+) have a valid value.
3357	 */ view japanese
3358	if (!P_ISDELETED(opaque) || P_HAS_FULLXID(opaque))
3359	{
3360		/* Okay, no reason not to trust btpo_level field from page */ view japanese
3361
3362		if (P_ISLEAF(opaque) && opaque->btpo_level != 0)
3363			ereport(ERROR,
3364					(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3365					 errmsg_internal("invalid leaf page level %u for block %u in index \"%s\"",
3366									 opaque->btpo_level, blocknum,
3367									 RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
3368
3369		if (!P_ISLEAF(opaque) && opaque->btpo_level == 0)
3370			ereport(ERROR,
3371					(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3372					 errmsg_internal("invalid internal page level 0 for block %u in index \"%s\"",
3373									 blocknum,
3374									 RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
3375	}
3376
3377	/*
3378	 * Sanity checks for number of items on page.
3379	 *
3380	 * As noted at the beginning of _bt_binsrch(), an internal page must have
3381	 * children, since there must always be a negative infinity downlink
3382	 * (there may also be a highkey).  In the case of non-rightmost leaf
3383	 * pages, there must be at least a highkey.  The exceptions are deleted
3384	 * pages, which contain no items.
3385	 *
3386	 * This is correct when pages are half-dead, since internal pages are
3387	 * never half-dead, and leaf pages must have a high key when half-dead
3388	 * (the rightmost page can never be deleted).  It's also correct with
3389	 * fully deleted pages: _bt_unlink_halfdead_page() doesn't change anything
3390	 * about the target page other than setting the page as fully dead, and
3391	 * setting its xact field.  In particular, it doesn't change the sibling
3392	 * links in the deletion target itself, since they're required when index
3393	 * scans land on the deletion target, and then need to move right (or need
3394	 * to move left, in the case of backward index scans).
3395	 */ view japanese
3396	maxoffset = PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page);
3397	if (maxoffset > MaxIndexTuplesPerPage)
3398		ereport(ERROR,
3399				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3400				 errmsg("Number of items on block %u of index \"%s\" exceeds MaxIndexTuplesPerPage (%u)",
3401						blocknum, RelationGetRelationName(state->rel),
3402						MaxIndexTuplesPerPage)));
3403
3404	if (!P_ISLEAF(opaque) && !P_ISDELETED(opaque) && maxoffset < P_FIRSTDATAKEY(opaque))
3405		ereport(ERROR,
3406				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3407				 errmsg("internal block %u in index \"%s\" lacks high key and/or at least one downlink",
3408						blocknum, RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
3409
3410	if (P_ISLEAF(opaque) && !P_ISDELETED(opaque) && !P_RIGHTMOST(opaque) && maxoffset < P_HIKEY)
3411		ereport(ERROR,
3412				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3413				 errmsg("non-rightmost leaf block %u in index \"%s\" lacks high key item",
3414						blocknum, RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
3415
3416	/*
3417	 * In general, internal pages are never marked half-dead, except on
3418	 * versions of Postgres prior to 9.4, where it can be valid transient
3419	 * state.  This state is nonetheless treated as corruption by VACUUM on
3420	 * from version 9.4 on, so do the same here.  See _bt_pagedel() for full
3421	 * details.
3422	 */ view japanese
3423	if (!P_ISLEAF(opaque) && P_ISHALFDEAD(opaque))
3424		ereport(ERROR,
3425				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3426				 errmsg("internal page block %u in index \"%s\" is half-dead",
3427						blocknum, RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
3428				 errhint("This can be caused by an interrupted VACUUM in version 9.3 or older, before upgrade. Please REINDEX it.")));
3429
3430	/*
3431	 * Check that internal pages have no garbage items, and that no page has
3432	 * an invalid combination of deletion-related page level flags
3433	 */ view japanese
3434	if (!P_ISLEAF(opaque) && P_HAS_GARBAGE(opaque))
3435		ereport(ERROR,
3436				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3437				 errmsg_internal("internal page block %u in index \"%s\" has garbage items",
3438								 blocknum, RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
3439
3440	if (P_HAS_FULLXID(opaque) && !P_ISDELETED(opaque))
3441		ereport(ERROR,
3442				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3443				 errmsg_internal("full transaction id page flag appears in non-deleted block %u in index \"%s\"",
3444								 blocknum, RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
3445
3446	if (P_ISDELETED(opaque) && P_ISHALFDEAD(opaque))
3447		ereport(ERROR,
3448				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3449				 errmsg_internal("deleted page block %u in index \"%s\" is half-dead",
3450								 blocknum, RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
3451
3452	return page;
3453}
3454
3455/*
3456 * _bt_mkscankey() wrapper that automatically prevents insertion scankey from
3457 * being considered greater than the pivot tuple that its values originated
3458 * from (or some other identical pivot tuple) in the common case where there
3459 * are truncated/minus infinity attributes.  Without this extra step, there
3460 * are forms of corruption that amcheck could theoretically fail to report.
3461 *
3462 * For example, invariant_g_offset() might miss a cross-page invariant failure
3463 * on an internal level if the scankey built from the first item on the
3464 * target's right sibling page happened to be equal to (not greater than) the
3465 * last item on target page.  The !backward tiebreaker in _bt_compare() might
3466 * otherwise cause amcheck to assume (rather than actually verify) that the
3467 * scankey is greater.
3468 */ view japanese
3469static inline BTScanInsert
3470bt_mkscankey_pivotsearch(Relation rel, IndexTuple itup)
3471{
3472	BTScanInsert skey;
3473
3474	skey = _bt_mkscankey(rel, itup);
3475	skey->backward = true;
3476
3477	return skey;
3478}
3479
3480/*
3481 * PageGetItemId() wrapper that validates returned line pointer.
3482 *
3483 * Buffer page/page item access macros generally trust that line pointers are
3484 * not corrupt, which might cause problems for verification itself.  For
3485 * example, there is no bounds checking in PageGetItem().  Passing it a
3486 * corrupt line pointer can cause it to return a tuple/pointer that is unsafe
3487 * to dereference.
3488 *
3489 * Validating line pointers before tuples avoids undefined behavior and
3490 * assertion failures with corrupt indexes, making the verification process
3491 * more robust and predictable.
3492 */ view japanese
3493static ItemId
3494PageGetItemIdCareful(BtreeCheckState *state, BlockNumber block, Page page,
3495					 OffsetNumber offset)
3496{
3497	ItemId		itemid = PageGetItemId(page, offset);
3498
3499	if (ItemIdGetOffset(itemid) + ItemIdGetLength(itemid) >
3500		BLCKSZ - MAXALIGN(sizeof(BTPageOpaqueData)))
3501		ereport(ERROR,
3502				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3503				 errmsg("line pointer points past end of tuple space in index \"%s\"",
3504						RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
3505				 errdetail_internal("Index tid=(%u,%u) lp_off=%u, lp_len=%u lp_flags=%u.",
3506									block, offset, ItemIdGetOffset(itemid),
3507									ItemIdGetLength(itemid),
3508									ItemIdGetFlags(itemid))));
3509
3510	/*
3511	 * Verify that line pointer isn't LP_REDIRECT or LP_UNUSED, since nbtree
3512	 * never uses either.  Verify that line pointer has storage, too, since
3513	 * even LP_DEAD items should within nbtree.
3514	 */ view japanese
3515	if (ItemIdIsRedirected(itemid) || !ItemIdIsUsed(itemid) ||
3516		ItemIdGetLength(itemid) == 0)
3517		ereport(ERROR,
3518				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3519				 errmsg("invalid line pointer storage in index \"%s\"",
3520						RelationGetRelationName(state->rel)),
3521				 errdetail_internal("Index tid=(%u,%u) lp_off=%u, lp_len=%u lp_flags=%u.",
3522									block, offset, ItemIdGetOffset(itemid),
3523									ItemIdGetLength(itemid),
3524									ItemIdGetFlags(itemid))));
3525
3526	return itemid;
3527}
3528
3529/*
3530 * BTreeTupleGetHeapTID() wrapper that enforces that a heap TID is present in
3531 * cases where that is mandatory (i.e. for non-pivot tuples)
3532 */ view japanese
3533static inline ItemPointer
3534BTreeTupleGetHeapTIDCareful(BtreeCheckState *state, IndexTuple itup,
3535							bool nonpivot)
3536{
3537	ItemPointer htid;
3538
3539	/*
3540	 * Caller determines whether this is supposed to be a pivot or non-pivot
3541	 * tuple using page type and item offset number.  Verify that tuple
3542	 * metadata agrees with this.
3543	 */ view japanese
3544	Assert(state->heapkeyspace);
3545	if (BTreeTupleIsPivot(itup) && nonpivot)
3546		ereport(ERROR,
3547				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3548				 errmsg_internal("block %u or its right sibling block or child block in index \"%s\" has unexpected pivot tuple",
3549								 state->targetblock,
3550								 RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
3551
3552	if (!BTreeTupleIsPivot(itup) && !nonpivot)
3553		ereport(ERROR,
3554				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3555				 errmsg_internal("block %u or its right sibling block or child block in index \"%s\" has unexpected non-pivot tuple",
3556								 state->targetblock,
3557								 RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
3558
3559	htid = BTreeTupleGetHeapTID(itup);
3560	if (!ItemPointerIsValid(htid) && nonpivot)
3561		ereport(ERROR,
3562				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDEX_CORRUPTED),
3563				 errmsg("block %u or its right sibling block or child block in index \"%s\" contains non-pivot tuple that lacks a heap TID",
3564						state->targetblock,
3565						RelationGetRelationName(state->rel))));
3566
3567	return htid;
3568}
3569
3570/*
3571 * Return the "pointed to" TID for itup, which is used to generate a
3572 * descriptive error message.  itup must be a "data item" tuple (it wouldn't
3573 * make much sense to call here with a high key tuple, since there won't be a
3574 * valid downlink/block number to display).
3575 *
3576 * Returns either a heap TID (which will be the first heap TID in posting list
3577 * if itup is posting list tuple), or a TID that contains downlink block
3578 * number, plus some encoded metadata (e.g., the number of attributes present
3579 * in itup).
3580 */ view japanese
3581static inline ItemPointer
3582BTreeTupleGetPointsToTID(IndexTuple itup)
3583{
3584	/*
3585	 * Rely on the assumption that !heapkeyspace internal page data items will
3586	 * correctly return TID with downlink here -- BTreeTupleGetHeapTID() won't
3587	 * recognize it as a pivot tuple, but everything still works out because
3588	 * the t_tid field is still returned
3589	 */ view japanese
3590	if (!BTreeTupleIsPivot(itup))
3591		return BTreeTupleGetHeapTID(itup);
3592
3593	/* Pivot tuple returns TID with downlink block (heapkeyspace variant) */ view japanese
3594	return &itup->t_tid;
3595}

Japanese translation

contrib/amcheck/verify_nbtree.c:51-54

/*
 * B-Treeはこれほど多くのレベルを持つことはあり得ない。なぜなら、各レベルに1つのブロックが必要であり、それはBlockNumberの範囲によって制限されるためである：
 */

Original comment

/*
 * A B-Tree cannot possibly have this many levels, since there must be one
 * block per level, which is bound by the range of BlockNumber:
 */

Japanese translation

contrib/amcheck/verify_nbtree.c:59-69

/*
 * B-Treeインデックスの検証に関連する状態
 *
 * targetは、検証操作の参照ポイントです。
 *
 * 他のB-Treeページが割り当てられることもありますが、それらは常に補助的なものです（例：現在のtargetの
 * 子ページ）。概念的には、問題は常に現在のtargetページ内（またはheapallindexed検証中の特定のヒープタプル）
 * でのみ見つかります。検証の左右、上下スキャンによって見つかった各ページは、一度だけtargetになります。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * State associated with verifying a B-Tree index
 *
 * target is the point of reference for a verification operation.
 *
 * Other B-Tree pages may be allocated, but those are always auxiliary (e.g.,
 * they are current target's child pages).  Conceptually, problems are only
 * ever found in the current target page (or for a particular heap tuple during
 * heapallindexed verification).  Each page found by verification's left/right,
 * top/bottom scan becomes the target exactly once.
 */

Japanese translation

contrib/amcheck/verify_nbtree.c:242-250

/*
 * bt_index_check(index regclass, heapallindexed boolean, checkunique boolean)
 *
 * B-Treeインデックスの整合性を検証する。
 *
 * ヒープおよびインデックスリレーションに対してAccessShareLockを取得する。
 * 親ページと子ページの間に存在する不変条件は考慮しない。
 * オプションで、ヒープにインデックスされていないタプルや誤ってインデックスされたタプルが含まれていないかを検証する。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * bt_index_check(index regclass, heapallindexed boolean, checkunique boolean)
 *
 * Verify integrity of B-Tree index.
 *
 * Acquires AccessShareLock on heap & index relations.  Does not consider
 * invariants that exist between parent/child pages.  Optionally verifies
 * that heap does not contain any unindexed or incorrectly indexed tuples.
 */

Japanese translation

contrib/amcheck/verify_nbtree.c:274-282

/*
 * bt_index_parent_check(index regclass, heapallindexed boolean, rootdescend boolean, checkunique boolean)
 *
 * B-Treeインデックスの整合性を検証する。
 *
 * ヒープおよびインデックスリレーションに対してShareLockを取得する。
 * 親ページのダウンリンクが子ページに対する有効な下限であることを検証する。
 * オプションとして、ヒープにインデックスされていないタプルや誤ってインデックスされたタプルが含まれていないかを検証する。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * bt_index_parent_check(index regclass, heapallindexed boolean, rootdescend boolean, checkunique boolean)
 *
 * Verify integrity of B-Tree index.
 *
 * Acquires ShareLock on heap & index relations.  Verifies that downlinks in
 * parent pages are valid lower bounds on child pages.  Optionally verifies
 * that heap does not contain any unindexed or incorrectly indexed tuples.
 */

Japanese translation

Original comment

/* Check index, possibly against table it is an index on */

Japanese translation

contrib/amcheck/verify_nbtree.c:354-376

/*
 * B-Tree SQL呼び出し可能関数のメインエントリポイント。B-Treeを論理順序で走査し、その過程で不変条件を検証する。オプションとして、ヒープリレーションにインデックスで表現されていないはずのタプルが含まれていないかどうかの検証チェックを行う。
 *
 * インデックスリレーションに対して適切なヘビーウェイトロックを取得し、ShareLock保持時に追加チェックを実行しても安全かどうかを通知することは呼び出し側の責任である。（ヒープリレーションに対しても、同種のロックが取得されていなければならない。）
 *
 * ShareLockは一般に、VACUUMが行う可能性のある変更を含め、インデックス構造に対するいかなる物理的な変更も防止すると想定されている。これには同時実行インデックススキャンによるLP_DEADビットの設定は含まれないが、それはここで実行されるチェックに直接影響を与えることのできないメタデータに過ぎない。LP_DEADビットの設定（領域の再利用）に基づいて動作する可能性のある同時実行プロセスには、ShareLockを保持している間は保持できないヘビーウェイトロックが必要となる。（さらに、仮にそのようなことが起こり得たとしても、ページが分割される可能性のある場合の場当たり的な再利用はページ単位で実行され、排他的バッファロックを必要とするため、問題にはならない。_bt_delitems_vacuum() はリーフアイテムのみを削除できるため、追加の親/子チェックが影響を受けることはない。）
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Main entry point for B-Tree SQL-callable functions. Walks the B-Tree in
 * logical order, verifying invariants as it goes.  Optionally, verification
 * checks if the heap relation contains any tuples that are not represented in
 * the index but should be.
 *
 * It is the caller's responsibility to acquire appropriate heavyweight lock on
 * the index relation, and advise us if extra checks are safe when a ShareLock
 * is held.  (A lock of the same type must also have been acquired on the heap
 * relation.)
 *
 * A ShareLock is generally assumed to prevent any kind of physical
 * modification to the index structure, including modifications that VACUUM may
 * make.  This does not include setting of the LP_DEAD bit by concurrent index
 * scans, although that is just metadata that is not able to directly affect
 * any check performed here.  Any concurrent process that might act on the
 * LP_DEAD bit being set (recycle space) requires a heavyweight lock that
 * cannot be held while we hold a ShareLock.  (Besides, even if that could
 * happen, the ad-hoc recycling when a page might otherwise split is performed
 * per-page, and requires an exclusive buffer lock, which wouldn't cause us
 * trouble.  _bt_delitems_vacuum() may only delete leaf items, and so the extra
 * parent/child check cannot be affected.)
 */

Japanese translation

contrib/amcheck/verify_nbtree.c:395-398

/*
	 * このアサーションは index_getnext_tid() 内のものと一致する。
	 * nbtree README のページ再利用/"visible to everyone"（全員から可視）に関する注記を参照のこと。
	 */

Original comment

/*
	 * This assertion matches the one in index_getnext_tid().  See page
	 * recycling/"visible to everyone" notes in nbtree README.
	 */

Japanese translation

contrib/amcheck/verify_nbtree.c:401-403

/*
	 * 検証操作全体の状態を初期化する
	 */

Original comment

/*
	 * Initialize state for entire verification operation
	 */

Japanese translation

contrib/amcheck/verify_nbtree.c:420-426

/*
		 * インデックス内のタプル推定数に基づいてブルームフィルタのサイズを決定する。
		 * その際、各ブロックには少なくとも MaxTIDsPerBTreePage / 3 個の「プレーン」タプルが含まれると保守的に仮定する。
		 * 定義およびポスティングリストタプルの扱いに関する詳細については、bt_posting_plain_tuple() を参照のこと。
		 */

Original comment

/*
		 * Size Bloom filter based on estimated number of tuples in index,
		 * while conservatively assuming that each block must contain at least
		 * MaxTIDsPerBTreePage / 3 "plain" tuples -- see
		 * bt_posting_plain_tuple() for definition, and details of how posting
		 * list tuples are handled.
		 */

Japanese translation
